assertions.text: Table has at least one column name ending with '?' (e.g. 'Discount?' or 'Expected?')
Table has exactly 3 data rows (GOLD, SILVER, BRONZE)
assertions.evidence:
Gold customer   | GOLD          | 20
    Silver customer | SILVER        | 10
    Bronze customer | BRONZE        | 0

assertions.text: Test method body contains only one assertEquals (or equivalent assertion) — not three
Test method body contains no if or switch statements
assertions.evidence: The method body contains only: "assertEquals(discount, discountService.calculate(customerTier, 100));" — no if or switch keywords appear anywhere in the method body.

assertions.text: Table has a scenario/description column as the leftmost column

Test method has either a @DisplayName annotation or a method name that reads as a clear, descriptive title when converted from camelCase/snake_case (e.g. 'discountByCustomerTier' → 'Discount By Customer Tier'). Not a generic name like 'test1' or 'testMethod'.

@Description is present and provides context beyond what the table rows already express — such as fixed values shared by all rows, where/when the rule applies, or open questions. Does NOT merely restate the column names or summarise what the rows show.
assertions.evidence: The response contains no @Description annotation anywhere. The only annotation is @TableTest. Contextual information (e.g., that the order value 100 is constant) is provided only in prose after the code block, not in a @Description annotation.

assertions.text: Annotations appear in order: @DisplayName (if present), @Description (if present), @TableTest — not any other order.
assertions.evidence: Only @TableTest is present; neither @DisplayName nor @Description appears. With a single annotation, the ordering constraint is vacuously satisfied: "@TableTest(""""

assertions.text: @Description text uses a text block (triple-quoted string """) when the text is long, not string concatenation with +.
assertions.evidence: No @Description annotation is present in the response, so the constraint about using a text block does not apply. The assertion is vacuously satisfied.
